text:
As much as I really want to see a rare disease like this be used in such a fun way, I have a really hard time just suspending reality in my brain for the sake of entertainment. It’s not just about pain being the limiting factor. Pain is a signal that warns us something is wrong and not to continue doing something that is causing damage. Great he doesn’t get that signal but it doesn’t mean his body isn’t being destroyed. Also, he crushes his hands. Now he doesn’t have pain but his fingers are still broken and crushed, the swelling is still going to happen and his ability to open and close his fist or use his fingers in any way (like the pincer motion index and thumb). His shattered hand has no structure anymore to do anything. His burned hand when it heals won’t have flexible skin and will lose mobility. The injuris like the arrow the bullet and the knife piercing him have introduced bacteria and now he’s going to have raging infections. The hand burn means theres no skin to protect and be a barrier to all the dirty stuff he’s grabbing. Not to mention if he pulls those out he bleeds out.
He has broken ribs and on penetrated a lung or he has internal bleeding. Okay he can keep going but at some point he his body isn’t going to have the blood flow it needs to even think or move or breathe. He will just pass out. 
The difference with Wolverine was that he actually healed. 
Say Hughie survives and saves the girl, now he’s in the hospital on a ton of antibiotics, surgeries like crazy trying to recreate a skeleton, burn care for his hands, depending on the infection and crush injuries he may not have blood flow and have to have amputation. And the physical and occupational therapy is going to take years for him to even get a 50% function. This man will barely be able to feed, bathe and clothe himself and might need a full-time caregiver.

text: Just removing the sensation of pain isn't a sufficient super power. Pain is basically body complaining that something is wrong. Burns from wielding the refried handgun will eventually turn septic, if untreated fatal. The knife slashing the hand, blood loss plus infection from open wound, fatal. That weird contraption that hit his back looks heavy and at the position to fracture his spine. Plus it has nails poking him. Also fatal. Like if you are sick and take a pain killer, even if the pain goes away you will feel better but you aren't always cured right away. My favorite tv show has dragons so I am being hypocritical by not suspending my disbelief but the plot needs additional armor here. Spider venom, strength serum, genetic mutation etc.

text: For 19 years of my life I did not feel pain. It wasn’t a fantasy that I would tell myself, it was a condition I had. I broke my toe and I could see the bone and I was not in pain. After my surgery I felt like walking out instead of being put into a wheelchair. And then I met the girl I am with now and I feel pain. Real, physical pain. It is so odd to feel what all people feel and it stresses me. Sometimes I wish I could not feel pain again. It was definitely an advantage in high school. Anyway, I think I’ll watch this movie so that I can feel the connection to something I had in the past, seems great too.

text: Fun Fact: These type of people exist in the world.  Its a congenitial defect of the body - specifically CIP (Congenital Insensitivity to Pain).  They literally are born with no pain sensors -- these sensors tell the brain to simulate pain.  Remember, pain is an important positive feature, not a flaw of the body.  Pain is generated by the brain -- it's not real, but simulated. We need it to survive.   It's very rare for these people to live past 9 years old.  It's not because of some disease.  It's because they don't understand that physical pain keeps you alive.  In their case, since they can't feel pain nor understand the concept of it, they almost always off themselves, unintentionally.
